programme.title: On the Case: Spreadsheet of Terror
programme.desc: When the body of a teenage girl is found in the Columbia River, police wonder if she was the victim of an accident or suicide. But the evidence reveals it was the beginning of disturbing murder.

programme.title: On the Case: Driven to Murder
programme.desc: A woman is at first thought to be the victim a deadly hit-and-run, until police find the car that killed her nearby, and it turns out to be her own.

programme.title: On the Case: Stacy's Story
programme.desc: July 30th, 1980. Judy Linzey returned home in Oxnard, California. As she unlocked the front door, she noticed drops of blood on her porch and hurried inside and called out to her teenage daughter but there was no answer.

programme.title: On the Case: Deadly Deception
programme.desc: Officers investigate the death of a father of four who was gunned down, however nothing about the crime is what it appears to be.

programme.title: On the Case: Blood, Sweat and Tears
programme.desc: A cold case team reopens the investigation into the murder of 36-year-old Linda Robinson who is savagely murdered while her seven-year-old niece is asleep in the next room.

programme.title: On the Case: A Secret Meeting
programme.desc: After a teenager mysteriously disappears, police must determine if a secret meeting with a young man led to her murder.

programme.title: On the Case: A Murder in Thunderbolt
programme.desc: When a couple is shot down in front of their home in the quiet suburb of Thunderbolt, Georgia, the entire community hears the barrage of gunfire. As police sift through the evidence, they must determine if one of the neighbors is a witness or the killer.

programme.title: On the Case: Murder in Room 205
programme.desc: A young woman's journey to a new life comes to a savage end in a roadside motel.

programme.title: On the Case: Kidnapped on the 4th of July
programme.desc: When a young woman's pizza delivery car is found abandoned in the middle of the desolate stretch of road, a twenty three year mystery begins.

programme.title: On the Case: Cali-Doe
programme.desc: A young girl is murdered in Caledonia New York, and police struggle to identify her. The tragedy leads the town make her one of their own, with the name Cali-Doe.

programme.title: On the Case: Driven to Kill
programme.desc: A new father comes home to find his infant son and wife missing. Four months later their bodies are found in a remote wooded area. When police investigate the father's past they find the reason for the unthinkable crime.

programme.title: On the Case: Deadly Paper Trail
programme.desc: The police follow a trail of emails, hoping it will lead them to a 38-year-old woman's vicious killer.

programme.title: On the Case: Left in the Dark
programme.desc: When a beautiful young graduate student is found stabbed to death, detectives must determine if the murder was a crime of passion or a brutal attack by a violent intruder.

programme.title: On the Case: Devil in a Cowboy Hat
programme.desc: A young woman is found bound and strangled to death on the side of a road. As police retrace her final steps, they discover she was last seen being followed by a suspicious man in a cowboy hat.

programme.title: On the Case: Twice Is No Accident
programme.desc: When Fran Smith mysteriously disappears her sister and daughter mount their own investigation... What they uncover sends police on a cross country man hunt for a killer.

programme.title: On the Case: Twisted Lies
programme.desc: In Las Vegas, 41-year-old Nancy Menke, a mother of four, is found handcuffed and murdered in the passenger seat of her car; a records clerk in the insurance fraud unit stumbles upon a strange report that blows open Menke's murder case.

programme.title: On the Case: In the Blink of an Eye
programme.desc: November 21, 1988, Sheriff's deputies received an alarming phone call from a convenience store in Leesville, Louisiana. A delivery person said the clerk who signs for deliveries was missing and the store's cash register was open and empty
programme.sub-title: In the Blink of an Eye

programme.title: On the Case: Unthinkable Harm
programme.desc: When a teenager is found savagely stabbed to death in her home, police hope a trail of blood found at the crime scene will lead them to her killer.

programme.title: On the Case: Last Dance
programme.desc: A woman's walk home from a night out at a dance club ends in a brutal murder.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: Rivals (REDUX)
programme.desc: Callie's irritation with the Trans-Utopian's ongoing technical problems is aggravated when the sleek bounty hunting ship Seattle and its debonair captain rescue the crew from a problem. They owe him, but the whole situation doesn't feel right.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: Heir and the Spare (REDUX)
programme.desc: The crew arrest a con-man preying on older women. But the prisoner has a dark secret and a genetic link to Marcus. Meanwhile, Percy's secret parallel search for Dante begins to wear on her; we fear for the consequences to her health and judgement.

programme.title: Dark Matter: Going Out Fighting
programme.desc: After collapsing in her quarters Two learns that her nanites are failing. In order to save her life, the crew of The Raza must pull off a dangerous op inside the headquarters of Dwarf Star Technologies.

programme.title: Dark Matter: Take the Shot
programme.desc: When the Android experiences a dream for the first time, she is compelled to inform the crew she may be malfunctioning. After members of the crew begin to be affected in strange ways, it becomes clear they are facing a larger threat.

programme.title: Dark Matter: Wish I'd Spaced You When I Had the Chance
programme.desc: While resupplying at a planetary outpost, Five is taken captive by a local gang of criminal brothers. Three races to locate her as the rest of the crew deal with Chief Inspector Kierken and the Galactic Authority.
programme.sub-title: Wish I'd Spaced You When I Had the Chance

programme.title: Dark Matter: Sometimes in Life You Don't Get to Choose
programme.desc: Having regained the memories of Ishida Ryo, Four is determined to reclaim the throne of Ishida. With the crew's help he uses the Blink Drive to persuade old friends to his cause.
programme.sub-title: Sometimes in Life You Don't Get to Choose

programme.title: Dark Matter: But First, We Save the Galaxy
programme.desc: With the prospect of an intergalactic war looming, the crew of The Raza races against time to thwart an attempt to destroy a spaces station hosting a corporate conference.
programme.sub-title: But First, We Save the Galaxy

programme.title: Dark Matter: Being Better Is So Much Better
programme.desc: In the season two finale the EOS 7 space station is destroyed while TWO, THREE, FIVE, and SIX are still onboard. TWO and SIX escape the blast in the Marauder, but the shuttle is damaged in the blast, leaving them floating dead in space.
programme.sub-title: Being Better Is So Much Better

programme.title: Dark Matter: It Doesn't Have To Be Like This
programme.desc: Now back on the Raza, the crew turns their attention to Ryo Ishida and the stolen Blink Drive. They track down the location of Ishida's secret research station, but something goes wrong when Two, Three, and Six access.
programme.sub-title: It Doesn't Have To Be Like This

programme.title: Dark Matter: Welcome to the Revolution
programme.desc: The Raza receives a distress call from their handler, Tabor, but when they arrive he's nowhere to be found. Instead his former assistant Adrian is waiting for them on an outer colony in the middle of a stand off with their corporate overloads.

programme.title: Dark Matter: All the Time in the World
programme.desc: Unbeknownst to the rest of the crew Three has been trapped in a time loop, reliving the same day over and over. He must convince his crew mates that what's happening to him is real and find a way to stop the loop.
programme.sub-title: All the Time in the World

programme.title: Dark Matter: Give It Up, Princess
programme.desc: A friend of Adrian's is taken captive by one of Tabor's rivals, demanding a specific data file in exchange for her release. The crew discover that the file contains information about the construction of a secret Ferrous battle fleet.

programme.title: Flash Gordon's Trip To Mars: Ming the Merciless
programme.desc: After Capturing Ming and Azura on the way to the Clay people, the merciless one unfolds his bat wings and escapes.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: Just Politics (REDUX)
programme.desc: Rudolfo becomes suspicious of a trade minister the Trans-Utopian is transport­ing to the Manheim Asteroid Belt to clinch a deal. Rudolfo will benefit from the deal, but it may be too good to be true.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: Negative Energy (REDUX)
programme.desc: Marcus believes he can convert the Trans-Utopian's hyper drive with negative energy instead of antimatter, rendering it safer, but hey will need Durenium to test it. He seeks a seller of this rare and - in some orbits - illegal, element.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: License to Fill (REDUX)
programme.desc: A vengeful bureaucrat has the Trans-Utopian crew charged for a license violation. Travis must defend himself in court, where his dubious past is revealed, placing him in grave danger.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: Hyperspace I (REDUX)
programme.desc: Marcus finds an obscure Divinity Cluster scientist who may hold a key to new ways of altering our relationship with space-time. He succeeds all too well.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: Hyperspace II (REDUX)
programme.desc: The Trans-Utopian's crew is trapped in separate folds of space-time with a ticking-clock to rejoin into one spacetime. Travis will meet Dante while Percy and Dante up a risky plan to extricate themselves from their respective hyperspatial traps.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: The Divinity Cluster (REDUX)
programme.desc: Bounty Hunter Dante Montana encounters a dangerous old "frenemy," while his partner, Luc, tracks a man who may hold the key to the Divinity Cluster, human genes with extraterrestrial origins.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: Trust (REDUX)
programme.desc: Dante's still impressionable teenage niece, Percy, comes under the sway of a young, handsome psychopath. Her actions inadvertently threaten the life of her uncle.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: Order (REDUX)
programme.desc: When a ship of religious cultists commit mass suicide by diving their ship into the sun, the crew rescues a shuttle full of survivors, including the cult leader himself, Brother Thirteen.

programme.title: Killer Town
programme.desc: A Halloween murder shocks the entire community of Armstrong, B.C. With little more than a composite sketch to go on, investigators work tirelessly to bring the killer to justice.

programme.title: Killer Town
programme.desc: When hardware store clerk Sydney Loofe vanishes after an online date, the entire state of Nebraska finds themselves on high alert. Investigators race against the clock to find her, as her family clutches onto the hope that Sydney is unharmed.

programme.title: Killer Britain
programme.desc: With a new baby and partner Ian back in her life, Kayleigh thought her life was complete. But his anger and controlling behaviour knew no bounds. Her family saw him as a ticking time bomb, and when he went off he took the life of a young mother.

programme.title: Killer Britain
programme.desc: Nathan Maynard Ellis and David Leelsey shared a love of the macabre - their home was a shrine to horror. Maynard Ellis had long fantasised about turning fiction into fact. When he met the unsuspecting Julia Rawson, her fate was sealed.

programme.title: I Am A Stalker
programme.desc: Convicted stalkers tell in their own words, how love turned into a deadly obsession in face first, captivating in-depth interviews.

programme.title: I Am A Stalker
programme.desc: Convicted stalkers tell in their own words, how love turned into a deadly obsession in face first, captivating in-depth interviews.

programme.title: I Am A Stalker
programme.desc: Convicted stalkers tell in their own words, how love turned into a deadly obsession in face first, captivating in-depth interviews.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Shipman
programme.desc: Doctor Harold Shipman was convicted of killing 15 patients in 2000, but he is thought to have killed up to 215. Discover how he was eventually caught by detectives.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Stephanie Slater
programme.desc: Estate agent Stephanie Slater reveals the story of her abduction, rape and incarceration, which occurred in 1992 whilst showing a man around a house.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Russell Murders
programme.desc: Bludgeoned to death whilst walking down a country lane, the horrific murders of Lin and Megan Russell made worldwide news. Discover the events of that shocking day.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Beverly Allitt
programme.desc: Beverley Allitt was thought to be a 'model' nurse. Very astute, caring and always willing to help, why did she murder four children and attempt to kill 11 others?

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The London Nail Bombings
programme.desc: London was brought to a standstill in April 1999 when 23-year-old political extremist David Copeland caused terror with a bombing campaign.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: Countryside Killings: Graham Backhouse
programme.desc: Robbie Coltrane examines why Graham Backhouse attempted to kill his wife with a car bomb before murdering his neighbour in 1984.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: Far From Home: Kyu Soo Kim
programme.desc: Robbie Coltrane examines the harrowing case of a London landlord who sadistically tortured and murdered two of his tenants.
programme.sub-title: Far From Home: Kyu Soo Kim

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: The Saturday Night Strangler: Joe Kappen
programme.desc: In 1973, three young girls were beaten, raped and strangled over a six-week period. The Saturday Night Strangler escaped justice for almost 30 years. But new techniques in DNA technology and a midnight exhumation finally uncovered the killer.
programme.sub-title: The Saturday Night Strangler: Joe Kappen

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Triangle Terror: For years, the flying saucer has been synonymous with UFO terror. But is it still the spacecraft that frightens us the most? Or has fear taken a new shape?

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Alien Achilles Heel. Many believe aliens are already here on Earth. Could they be plotting an attack? And if so, can humans survive their weapons' terrifying capabilities?

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Signs of Abduction: From loss of time and bizarre hazy memories, to terrifying accounts of alien surgery, these are the hidden signs of alien abduction.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Countdown To Disclosure: Evidence suggests the world's governments could be on the verge of announcing everything they know about UFOs. What will this mean for our society?

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: Anti-Christ?
programme.desc: Every culture and religion on the planet believes that an evil one will usher in the end of days. But who is this Anti-Christ? And how can we hope to stop him?

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: Stargate Portals
programme.desc: Discover doorways to wormholes in time, their secret locations around the world, as well as who is trying to open them and why.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: America's Alien Wars
programme.desc: Has our government been at war with alien aggressors for the past half century? Startling evidence indicates that the US military has been under attack and has kept it from the public.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: America's Flying Saucer
programme.desc: Discover America's new killer aircraft and the out-of-this-world technology the US military uses to conquer the globe.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: Top Ten Secret Societies
programme.desc: An elite group of power players may be plotting the fate of the planet. Discover the secrets behind ten powerful hidden organisations around the world.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: Fake World Leaders
programme.desc: Fake World Leaders: Are Hitler, Saddam Hussein and Osama Bin Laden really dead? Experts find proof that several infamous world leaders didn't die when you think they did.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: Global Depopulation
programme.desc: Disasters, famine and pandemics threaten civilisation on a global scale. A cataclysmic event may spell the end of civilisation and the human race could face extinction.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: Time Travel
programme.desc: Evidence suggests that time travel may have occurred in the past.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: Nazis Today
programme.desc: For decades, there have been rumours key members of Hitler’s inner circle escaped the fall of Berlin - maybe even the Führer himself. But where did they go and what is their plan?

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: Remote Viewing
programme.desc: Documents may prove that the government has a secret group of remote viewers watching its citizens: the CIA's 'psy ops army'.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: Secret Alien Attack
programme.desc: Secret satellites. Death stars. The weaponisation of outer space. Are governments the world over preparing for an alien attack? And if so, why are we being kept in the dark?

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: Secret Government Warehouses
programme.desc: From Fort Knox to Hangar 18 and Camp Hero, discover a series of top-secret and high-security national database centres.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: Doomsday
programme.desc: There have been doomsday signs and prophecies for centuries, and humans' time on this planet may be nearly over.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: Bio Hazard Island
programme.desc: A secret government facility may have created many of the diseases currently plaguing the world.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: New World Order
programme.desc: An elite group of power-brokers may be trying to rule the planet.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: Mind Control
programme.desc: There are those who wish to control what others think, how they act and what they believe. Discover the new practices in mind-control.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: Zombies
programme.desc: This episode explores what would happen if a zombie outbreak ever became a reality.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: Rise Of The Machines
programme.desc: If technology goes too far, too fast, a time may come when humans are no longer at the top of the evolutionary food chain.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: Cloning
programme.desc: Disturbing experiments in animal cloning go on behind closed doors and human cloning may soon be a reality.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: Bigfoot
programme.desc: The evidence for Bigfoot piles up, causing some to wonder who has been keeping information from the public.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: Top 10 Conspiracies Of All Time
programme.desc: Those in power have secrets they do not want average citizens to know.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: Haarp
programme.desc: Governments around the world may be using weather control to incite the next World War and possibly destroy the planet.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: Anti-Christ?
programme.desc: Every culture and religion on the planet believes that an evil one will usher in the end of days. But who is this Anti-Christ? And how can we hope to stop him?

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Freak of the Deep: Deep-sea explorers discover a monster thousands of feet deep in the ocean, killer fog threatens a US town, and a truck spontaneously explodes on a freeway.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Doomsday at Yellowstone: Scientists seek to explain the unexplainable while investigating blue fire near Yellowstone, turkeys circling a dead cat and a Gettysberg ghost.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Curse of the Mayan Treasure: An explorer at a Mayan sacrificial site is attacked by an underwater fog, thousands of fish jump to their deaths, and a UFO is spotted in LA skies.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Gateway to Hell: A fire-breathing crater burns in the middle of a desert, a deadly shark lurks on a golf course, and a giant white blob threatens a town in California.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Return of the Ice Monster: A giant ice creature is spotted in Alaska, a jet has a close encounter with a UFO, and evidence of a lightning strike invites paranormal speculation.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Wrath of Thor: A mysterious flame appears in the Norwegian wilderness and video footage shows a ghostly figure stalking people in Japan, confirming a local legend.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: The Beast That Ate Jaws: A shark is eaten by a mysterious predator, and experts wonder if a prehistoric monster is to blame. A sinister sight is filmed near Russia's Area 51.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: The Incident at Area 51: Near Area 51, a police convoy is filmed transporting an alien-looking craft in the dead of night. Strange beams of light are reported in Argentina.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: The Omen of Blood River: A river of blood washes through a city in Bangladesh, an ominous noise scares a Canadian town and a giant hole appears in the clouds above California.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Siblings
programme.desc: Alexis gives her newfound long-lost son Adam a job at Colbyco. Blake starts to believe that Adam may actually be his real son. Fallon and Adam keep flirting with each other, not knowing they are siblings. Adam has dinner with Krystle and Blake.

programme.title: Dynasty: Mark
programme.desc: Blake tries to be friends with Adam, but Adam rejects him. Alexis tells Mark that he's still married to Krystle. Mark seeks to get a job as a tennis pro at Fallon's hotel.

programme.title: Dynasty: Kirby
programme.desc: Joseph's daughter, Kirby comes to stay with the Carringtons after breaking up with her fiance in France. Joseph tries to persuade her to go back to him. Mark gets the job at Fallon's hotel. Blake tries to get help from congressman Neal McVane.

programme.title: Dynasty: La Mirage
programme.desc: Fallon gets a letter from Steven and it turns out he's on an oil rig in the Java Sea, Indonesia. Adam mixes the toxic compound in the paint for Jeff's new office. Fallon hires Kirby as a nanny for little Blake.

programme.title: Dynasty: Acapulco
programme.desc: Jeff starts to become dizzy and affected by the toxic paint Adam used in his office. Joseph tells Kirby to stay away from Jeff. Krystle goes to Acapulco to find out what really happened with so-called divorce from Mark.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Locket
programme.desc: The Carrington family is informed that Steven's oil rig exploded. Blake goes to look for him, with an uninvited Alexis. Sammy Jo's father returns to Denver to collect Sammy Jo's share of Steven's inheritance, but Krystle throws him out.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Search
programme.desc: Alexis and Blake continue the search for Steven in Idonesia, but find evidence that Steven might be dead. Blake refuses to believe it, and hires a psychic, Jason Denner, to help. Jeff gets really ill from the paint in his office. Adam rapes Kirby.

programme.title: Dynasty: Samantha
programme.desc: Back in Denver, Blake continues his frantic search for Steven with the help of psychic Jason Dehner. Blake refuses to believe his son is dead, and Krystle is worried about his erratic behavior.Fallon mends fences with stepmother, Krystle.

programme.title: Dynasty: Danny
programme.desc: An obviously ill Jeff continues to function in a confused state. Sammy Jo announces she's headed for New York to make it big as a model and no longer wants to keep her son, Danny. Blake offers the girl $100,000 to be a mother to the child.

programme.title: Dynasty: Madness
programme.desc: Jeff's temper continues to grow more volatile. Adam attempts to implicate Jeff in the Cecil Colby/Logan Rhinewood scandal. After learning that Blake and Krystle intend to adopt Steven's son Danny, Alexis demands custody of her grandson.

programme.title: Dynasty: Two Flights to Haiti
programme.desc: Although Blake begs her to postpone taking action until Jeff's violent behavior can be explained. Fallon flies to Haiti to obtain a divorce; Mark shows up unexpectedly at her hotel. Alexis tells Adam she cannot allow Jeff to be poisoned.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Mirror
programme.desc: Alexis is concerned the doctors will learn the truth behind Jeff's illness, but Adam tries to cover by ordering his office remodeled and the lethal paint compound removed. Mark and Fallon get closer in Haiti.

programme.title: Dynasty: Battle Lines
programme.desc: Krystle suspects Alexis knows the reason for Jeff's illness; Blake tries to to prevent Alexis' proposed merger; Jeff is released from the hospital and Kirby reveals her feelings for him.

programme.title: Dynasty: Reunion in Singapore
programme.desc: As Blake is departing for Singapore to identify his son, Alexis intercepts him at the airport to assure him she will not be stopped from merging their respective companies.

programme.title: Dynasty: Fathers and Sons
programme.desc: Blake persaudes Steven to return to Denver for his son's sake; the rest of Carringtons are emotional learning that Steven's alive and returning home; once home, Steven reunites with Alexis.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Downstairs Bride
programme.desc: Upon hearing that Kirby has married Jeff, Joseph tells his daughter she has ruined her life. Blake suggests to Alexis they call a truce to their battle over Steven; Alexis offers Steven a share in Colbyco.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Vote
programme.desc: As the Board of Directors gathers to vote on the Denver-Carrington/Colbyco merger, Alexis pointedly tells the group that a nay vote will be met with immediate dismissal. The approval of the motion sends Blake into a fury.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Dinner
programme.desc: Blake confronts Alexis with evidence of fraudulent figures involved in the recent merger; Alexis learns Blake's lucrative oil extraction process does not extend to Colbyco. Newlyweds Jeff and Kirby have their first fight.

programme.title: Arthur: I Wanna Hold Your Hand/Whistling in the Wind
programme.desc: During a shopping trip to the mall, Binky gets caught doing the uncoolest of all the uncool things in the world--holding his mom's hand! If the Tibbles are supposed to be exactly the same, why can Tommy whistle while Timmy can't?
programme.sub-title: I Wanna Hold Your Hand/Whistling in the Wind

programme.title: Arthur: The Boy With His Head In The Clouds/More!
programme.desc: All the kids know that George is smart--so why does he sometimes have trouble reading and writing? Then, D.W. is thrilled to be getting an allowance. At last, she'll be rich and powerful!
programme.sub-title: The Boy With His Head In The Clouds/More!

programme.title: Arthur: Fright Night/Citizen Cheikh
programme.desc: Buster’s Uncle Bob – portrayed by special guest R.L. Stine – comes to visit and tells Buster a spooky bedtime story. Then, Cheikh learns that he and his family will become American citizens!

programme.title: Arthur: April 9th
programme.desc: April 9th is a normal day until a fire destroys the teacher's lounge and forces Lakewood Elementary to close. This unexpected event finds creates a wide range of emotional reactions, for Arthur, his friends and teachers, and the community.

programme.title: Arthur: Do You Speak George?/World Girls
programme.desc: Everyone at school is speaking their own made-up language, and George can't make sense of what anyone is saying. And it's the grand opening of World Girl World, which sells dolls from every country, and every girl in town is thrilled!

programme.title: Arthur: The Friend Who Wasn't There/Surprise!
programme.desc: Muffy embarks on a quest to find her long lost imaginary friend. And then it's Catherine's 16th birthday, and Francine has been cordially UN-invited to the party.

programme.title: Arthur: D.W.'S Library Card/Arthur's Big Hit
programme.desc: When D.W. discovers that she can get her own library card, she rejoices at the world of books waiting for her. And, is it ever okay to hit someone? Sometimes you just get so mad, you wanna....pow! Right??

programme.title: Arthur: Ants in Arthur's Pants/Don't Ask Muffy
programme.desc: To excel in science, the class has to prove that they have P.A.N.T.S.--the ability to be patient, attentive, nosy, thoughtful, and systematic. And Muffy starts an advice column to launch her career as a rich and famous talk-show host.
programme.sub-title: Ants in Arthur's Pants/Don't Ask Muffy

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur Plays The Blues/Buster's Sweet Success
programme.desc: Arthur's beloved piano teacher is retiring, but she assures him that the new teacher is outstanding--and famous, too! When Buster signs up to sell candy for the band, he figures it's easy money -- who doesn't love chocolate?
programme.sub-title: Arthur Plays The Blues/Buster's Sweet Success

programme.title: Arthur: Castles in the Sky/Tipping the Scales
programme.desc: The gang is devastated after their tree house collapses. Can architect Frank Gehry help them to rebuild? Everyone in Arthur's chorus group is excited about their upcoming performance in Crown City.
programme.sub-title: Castles in the Sky/Tipping the Scales

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: There is a virus spreading through Lakewood Elementary--it’s new nicknames and it’s changing how everyone treats each other! The playground has been quiet lately--too quiet--and the Tough Customers don't like it one bit.
programme.sub-title: Nicked by a Name/The Play's the Thing

programme.title: Arthur: My Dad, The Garbage Man/Poor Muffy
programme.desc: Mr. Ratburn's class is taking a field trips to visit parents at their jobs. Francine's not sure she wants the class to know her dad is a garbage man. And a new carpet and a sneezing attack means Muffy must spend the weekend at Francine's.
programme.sub-title: My Dad, The Garbage Man/Poor Muffy

programme.title: Arthur: D.W.'s Snow Mystery/Team Trouble
programme.desc: D.W. saves everything. But her most prized possession is a snowball from last winter. But now it’s disappeared! Then, Arthur, Francine, and Buster must do a report on ancient Rome--but can they work as a team to complete the assignment?

programme.title: Arthur: The Last Tough Customer/Brain's Chess Mess
programme.desc: The Tough Customers realize it's time to quit bullying and set out to find a new hobby--but can Molly ever truly change her ways? And Brain starts a chess club at school to improve his game and beat Los Dedos, the local chess master.
programme.sub-title: The Last Tough Customer/Brain's Chess Mess

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur's Baby/D.W.'s Baby
programme.desc: Arthur is not too sure he's ready for a new baby in the house. What do babies do besides cry? Then, living with a baby is no picnic. Just how long will Baby Kate be hanging around, anyway? And who would even miss D.W. if she ran away?

programme.title: Arthur: The Hallway Minotaur/Ladonna's Like List
programme.desc: Attention Lakewood Elementary! There's a new sheriff—er--hallway monitor-in town, and his name is George Lundgren. Ladonna Compson is friendly, funny, and full of personality. Everyone likes her! At least, that's what she thought.

programme.title: Arthur: Lost!/The Short, Quick Summer
programme.desc: One day, Arthur falls asleep on a bus and awakens in a strange part of town. Will he ever find his way home? Can he keep himself from crying? Next, summer vacation is over? What about all those great, fun things Arthur intended to do?

programme.title: Arthur: The Tattletale Frog/D.W. & Bud's Higher Purpose
programme.desc: When Bud is blamed for an accident D.W. causes, she has to decide whether to let her friend take the fall. D.W. and Bud can't wait to ride a new rollercoaster. Will they make it past the ticket taker, or will they come up short?
programme.sub-title: The Tattletale Frog/D.W. & Bud's Higher Purpose

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur's Almost Boring Day/The Half-Baked Sale
programme.desc: It's raining and boy is it BORING! But then, in Grandma Thora's attic, Arthur and D.W. discover some really FASCINATING stuff about their parents. Next, Grandma Thora is helping to organize a bake sale, but her goodies aren’t very good.
programme.sub-title: Arthur's Almost Boring Day/The Half-Baked Sale

programme.title: Arthur: Sleep No More/Pet Peeved
programme.desc: Buster is convinced that Pizza Paula's all-you-can-eat contest is his destiny. But how can he possibly win such an important contest without any sleep? Binky volunteers to look after Francine's cat, Nemo, for the weekend.

programme.title: Arthur: Pets and Pests/Go Fly a Kite
programme.desc: There's a mouse loose in the Read home! And D.W. wants it gone…NOW! When traps fail, Arthur enlists the help of an expert mouse catcher. While playing in the park, Binky, Muffy, and Ladonna stumble upon an abandoned kite.
programme.sub-title: Pets and Pests/Go Fly a Kite

programme.title: Arthur: The World Record/The Cave
programme.desc: Why is Buster walking backwards and Muffy not talking? They're trying to set world records of course! Then, Arthur thinks his class trip to the Clark Caves will be really cool--but won't it be dark in there?

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur Rides The Bandwagon/Dad's Dessert Dilemma
programme.desc: Woogles have their own names and personalities and they're so darned cute! And Arthur's gotta have one, especially now that everyone is collecting them. Arthur wouldn't mind if Dad's infamous dessert "experiments" remained a family secret.
programme.sub-title: Arthur Rides The Bandwagon/Dad's Dessert Dilemma

programme.title: Arthur: Prunella Sees the Light/Return of the Snowball
programme.desc: For her sleepover with Marina, Prunella redecorates her room. But Marina is blind--will the decorations she can't see offend her, or worse, make her trip? D.W. has been trying to solve the mystery of her snowball's disappearance.
programme.sub-title: Prunella Sees the Light/Return of the Snowball

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur and D.W. Clean Up/The Long, Dull Winter
programme.desc: Arthur just might make it to Happy Crazy Wow Day at the park--if he and D.W. can work together to clean their rooms! Then, Arthur and the gang realize they are suffering from the January doldrums brought on by an acute lack of holidays.
programme.sub-title: Arthur and D.W. Clean Up/The Long, Dull Winter

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur Unravels/All the Rage
programme.desc: Grandma Thora teaches Arthur to knit and he's really into this new hobby. But…he's a kid! And a boy! Knitting is a grandma thing, right? And, Muffy wants to rock the fashion world, but creating a fad is a lot harder than it looks.

programme.title: Arthur: Muffy's Classy Classics Club/Best Enemies
programme.desc: Muffy buys all of the books and dolls in the Pretty Pioneers series and invites her friends to join a pretty book club. Mom has new clients coming over and they have a daughter who is D.W.'s age. They're going to be the best of friends!

programme.title: Arthur: The "A" Team/Emily Swallows a Horse
programme.desc: Francine and Brain are the best players on the soccer team, and they wish their teammates could take the game a bit more seriously. And then Emily really wants the sparkly ball D.W. just found, so she tells a little white lie about it.

programme.title: Arthur: Big Brother Binky, Parts I&II
programme.desc: Binky's family has decided to adopt a little girl from China. The thought of a baby sister makes Binky happy, until he realized there are some changes in store. It’s a lot harder than he thought, but it has its rewards too.

programme.title: Arthur: For the Birds/Ungifted
programme.desc: Brain dreams of being the first to spot a rare bird species called the green-tailed grebe, making him famous. Arthur and Buster's friendship is put to the test when Arthur gives away a present that Buster had given him for his birthday.

programme.title: Arthur: So Long, Spanky/Buster's New Friend
programme.desc: D.W.'s beloved bird gets very sick, and despite D.W.'s eager ministrations, Spanky dies. Can she risk giving her heart to another pet? Then, Buster doesn't have time for Arthur. And all he wants to talk about is a new friend named Mike.

programme.title: Arthur: Postcards from Buster, Parts I & II
programme.desc: Buster's going to New York City to meet his dad Bo, and the Reads are coming with him! He's thrilled ... but won't his mom miss him? But, Bitzi gives him a present that will let him share his exciting trip with her--a video camera!
programme.sub-title: Postcards from Buster, Parts I & II

programme.title: Arthur: To Tibble the Truth/Waiting to Go
programme.desc: When the Tibble Twins' constant lying lands them in constant trouble, they promise to tell only the truth. Binky and Brain collide on the soccer field, breaking Brain's new watch and nearly breaking their friendship.
programme.sub-title: To Tibble the Truth/Waiting to Go

programme.title: BATTLEFRONT: WWII ROAD TO VICTORY: U-Boat War
programme.desc: U-boats posed the deadliest threat to Allied convoys traveling the Atlantic.

programme.title: Allied Commanders of WWII
programme.desc: Heroism was not unique to US commanders, these generals battled the Nazis on all fronts. A look at Bernard Law Montgomery, Georgi Zhukov, and Sir Arthur Harris.

programme.title: Allied Commanders of WWII
programme.desc: The commanders of World War II who led on the high seas and on the homefront. Charles Nimitz, George Marshall, and Joseph Stillwell are seen in profile.

programme.title: Allied Commanders of WWII
programme.desc: From Island-hopping to thirty seconds over Tokyo, this is a look at the battles of Douglas MacArthur, Hap Arnold, and James Doolittle.

programme.title: Hitler & The Nazis
programme.desc: The Treaty of Versailles and the Weimar Republic: Explores the signing of the Treaty of Versailles and its relation to the rise of Adolf Hitler.

programme.title: Hitler & The Nazis
programme.desc: Supreme Commander: Explores the Berlin Olympic Games and the rise of Adolf Hitler as the Supreme Commander of the German Reich.

programme.title: Hitler & The Nazis
programme.desc: Conquering France: Examines the Conquest of France in 1940 by Hitler's forces.

programme.title: Hitler & The Nazis
programme.desc: Peak of Power: Documenting the peak of Hitler's power and his plan to invade Great Britain.

programme.title: Hitler & The Nazis
programme.desc: The fall of a hate-fueled and manipulative madman, and those who faithfully followed him from the invasion at Normandy, the death camps of Auschwitz-Birkenau, the military failure at Stalingrad, and the fall of Berlin.

programme.title: World War I in the Trenches
programme.desc: Experience first hand "the war to end all wars." WWI was the first war in history to affect most of the globe, with battles fought on land, at sea and, for the first time, in the sky.
programme.sub-title: World War I in the Trenches - Episode 001

programme.title: World War I in the Trenches
programme.desc: WW1 starts with the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand of Austria-Hungary in 1914, in Sarajevo. The assassination triggered a chain of events as Austria-Hungary declared war on Serbia, leading to a series of treaties being activated.
programme.sub-title: World War I in the Trenches - Episode 002

programme.title: World War I in the Trenches
programme.desc: The conflict sawa- the development of trench warfare, with major battles such as the Battle of the Marne, the First Battle of Ypres, and the Race to the Sea, leading to a stalemate that set the stage for the prolonged and devastating nature of the war.
programme.sub-title: World War I in the Trenches - Episode 003

programme.title: World War I in the Trenches
programme.desc: The conflict saw- the development of trench warfare, with major battles such as the Battle of the Marne, the First Battle of Ypres, and the Race to the Sea, leading to a stalemate that set the stage for the prolonged and devastating nature of the war.
programme.sub-title: World War I in the Trenches - Episode 004

programme.title: World War I in the Trenches
programme.desc: The air war during WWI was significant as it marked the first widespread use of aircraft in combat, introducing reconnaissance, aerial dogfights, and strategic bombing.
programme.sub-title: World War I in the Trenches - Episode 005

programme.title: World War I in the Trenches
programme.desc: The year 1916 was marked by significant and brutal battles on the Western Front during World War I.
programme.sub-title: World War I in the Trenches - Episode 006

programme.title: World War I in the Trenches
programme.desc: During World War I, Turkey, then the Ottoman Empire, initially maintained neutrality but later joined the Central Powers (Germany and Austria-Hungary) in November 1914.
programme.sub-title: World War I in the Trenches - Episode 007

programme.title: World War I in the Trenches
programme.desc: World War I came to an end with the signing of the Armistice on November 11, 1918. The entry of the United States into the war on the side of the Allies, the balance of power shifted decisively.
programme.sub-title: World War I in the Trenches - Episode 008

programme.title: WWII In Color: From D-Day to Nazi Downfall
programme.desc: For nearly five years, Hitler's Third Reich has dominated Europe. Germany braces for an Allied invasion, fortifying the Atlantic Wall with steel, concrete, and advanced weaponry.

programme.title: WWII In Color: From D-Day to Nazi Downfall
programme.desc: After 18 months of preparation, the Allied Expeditionary Force launches the largest amphibious landing in history, with 160,000 troops storming the Normandy coast.

programme.title: WWII In Color: From D-Day to Nazi Downfall
programme.desc: After a brutal day of relentless combat, the Allies secure a 50-mile bridgehead in Normandy.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: Southern sweetheart Kelsie is madly in love with her online Romeo, Brandon. But after years of endless excuses about meeting up, Kelsie recruits Nev and Max to find who Brandon really is. (S6, ep14)

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: It's been seven years since April met Dean in a chat room at the age of thirteen. During that time, Dean would often disappear for months at a time. Can Nev and Max help April find Dean? (S6, ep15)

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Miracle & Javonni
programme.desc: Smart, single mum Miracle fell for Javonni, a dreamy-eyed Instagrammer. With Max on hiatus, Nev teams up with TV personality Charlamagne to take Miracle on a hunt for the man of her dreams.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Courtney & Isaak
programme.desc: Courtney broke up with Isaak, who she met online. He's doing everything to get her back. Beauty queen Cassidy Wolf joins Nev to get Isaak to leave Courtney alone for good.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Harold & Armani
programme.desc: Harold found his match with a beautiful fellow jock named Armani. Although they live in the same area, they've never met. Nev is joined by Angel Haze to help Harold get his perfect girl.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Daisy & Marcus
programme.desc: Daisy met Marcus on Instagram and he instantly stole her heart. But Marcus won't speak to Daisy on the phone, let alone meet her in person, so Nev and Tyler try to arrange a face to face.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Chitara & Priscilla
programme.desc: Chitara took a big leap and came out to her family when she met Priscilla online, but Priscilla ended their relationship unexpectedly. Now Chitara is determined to find out what happened.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Felipe & Jasmine
programme.desc: Single father Felipe is ready to settle down with Jasmine. However, the two have been talking online for eight months and have never met. Max returns and joins Nev to investigate.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Blaire & Markie
programme.desc: When Blaire's Instagram lover Markie broke her heart, she moved on. But now Markie is back and wants to meet. Blaire needs Nev and Max's help to make it happen and reveal Markie's secret.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: Sydney and Ayissha were in an online romance for years before Sydney revealed she is not who she says. But Ayissha still loves her and seeks Nev and Max's help.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: For shy Devan, meeting Rylan was love at first click. Devan wants to believe that Rylan is marriage material but her suspicious behaviour is leaving him with doubts.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Falesha & Jacqueline
programme.desc: Falesha's name and pictures were stolen by a fake profile that nearly ruined her life. She thought her troubles were behind her, but four years later her past comes back to haunt her.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: After being paralysed as a teenager, Robert met Ashleigh online and they fell in love. With news he may not have much longer to live, Robert asks Nev and Max to bring Ashleigh to him. (S6, ep16)

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: Robin is desperate to meet her hunky, long-distance love in person. Nev and Max agree to help her hunt him down, but what they uncover turns out to be much more than Robin bargained for! (S6, ep17)

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: For years, Nicole has had her pictures stolen to make fake profiles, one of which has been catfishing Ryan for ten years. Ryan and Nicole are unsure whether it's a random troll, or they have a common enemy.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: Jasmine writes to Nev and Max asking for help for a "friend". But they're perplexed when Jasmine drops a bombshell revelation: they've helped her friend before! (S6, ep19)

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Sunny & Jamison
programme.desc: Sunny is in love with the man of her dreams. Jamison is a model, a TV writer, and also studying to be an anaesthesiologist. However, the pair have never met. Is Jamison too good to be true?

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Trina & Scorpio
programme.desc: Trina is a dancer who always had trouble meeting trustworthy guys, until she met Scorpio, a fellow dancer, online. Will Trina finally get to rendezvous with the only man she's ever loved? (S1, ep2)

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Kim & Matt
programme.desc: Kim's been pouring her heart out to Matt for over 10 years. Now, Kim's facing a life-changing decision - should she marry her current boyfriend or find out if Matt is her soul mate?

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Jasmine & Mike
programme.desc: Jasmine has been texting with Mike for two years and thinks he could be the perfect step-father for her son. Although they live only 15 minutes apart, the pair have yet to meet up in person.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Jarrod & Abby
programme.desc: How do young people who've embraced the digital age find love? Often by connecting online with others they've never met in person before. The problem -- that cute guy you may be falling in love with could be lying about who he really is.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Bitter Pill
programme.desc: Stella Nickell of Auburn, Washington, was sentenced to 90 year in federal prison for poisoning her husband Bruce and Sue Snow, a Seattle bank manager with cyanide-laced Excedrin.

programme.title: 48 Hours: The Mystery of Slide Mountain
programme.desc: A report that examines evidence and unanswered questions regarding a June 1, 1998 auto accident on Slide Mountain, near Reno, Nevada, that led to the death of Rinette Riella Bergna and left the driver, Rinette’s husband Peter with only minor injuries.

programme.title: 48 Hours: The Colonel's Wife
programme.desc: Ret. Col. George Maracek, a 69-year-old former Green Beret who served in Korea and Vietnam, has been charged with the 1991 murder of his Thai wife Viparet. He maintains that he is innocent.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Reasonable Doubt
programme.desc: In September 1999, Brian Eftenoff of Phoenix, Az. Was convicted of murdering his wife, Judi. It was alleged that Eftenoff forced more than a gram of cocaine down his wife’s throat causing her to die of a cocaine-induced stroke.

programme.title: 48 Hours: The Babysitter
programme.desc: Chris Routh, a responsible 14-year-old honor student loved children and earned money as a babysitter.. On July 25, 2001, Emily a child in his care suddenly stopped breathing. Was this a case of murder, an accident, or natural causes. Police investigate.

programme.title: 48 Hours: While Innocents Slept
programme.desc: A report based on the murder case involving the deaths of two infants which were originally attributed to SIDS (Sudden Infant Death Syndrome).

programme.title: 48 Hours: Millionaire Boys Club
programme.desc: 48 Hours is television's most popular true-crime series, investigating shocking cases and compelling real-life dramas with journalistic integrity and cutting-edge style.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Mystery in Room 813
programme.desc: On November 13, 1996, at a Sheraton hotel east of Los Angeles, 27-year-old Sandra Orellana of Houston, on a business trip with her 33-year-old boss Robert Salazar, went over a balcony and met her death.

programme.title: 48 Hours: The Negotiators
programme.desc: An inside look at 23-year-old Jarrett Jordan, a disturbed man who shot three people and barricaded himself in a house in Queens, NY. He had with him his four-month-old daughter and three hostages.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Prisoners in Paradise
programme.desc: When Lois McMillen, a Connecticut artist was found dead on the British Virgin Island of Tortola in January 2000, four young American tourists were charged with her brutal murder.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Precious Angels
programme.desc: A report on the brutal murders in a Dallas, Texas suburb of Darlie and Darin Routier’s two sons -- Devon, 6 and Damon, 5. Darlie who claims that the boys were killed by an intruder, suffered serious knife wounds to her throat.

programme.title: 48 Hours: To Hell and Back
programme.desc: An interview with 17-year-old Jacque Marris who reveals new details about her abduction and how she and Tamara Brooks, another teenage victim, survived a harrowing 12-hour kidnapping on Aug. 1, 2002.

programme.title: 48 Hours: To Catch a Stalker
programme.desc: Fifteen-year-old Penny Chang of Shaker Heights, Ohio was stalked and killed by her older brother’s best friend, 21-year-old Scott Strothers.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Prime Suspect
programme.desc: Billy Fox, 33, is a devoted father of two and a soccer coach. He appears to be an all-around regular guy. But he’s booked for the death of a baby. It's a charge that could put him in prison for the rest of his life.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Web of Seduction
programme.desc: A report on problems which arise when children under the age of 17 receive unwanted sexual letters on the Internet, focusing on Lindsay, a 14-year-old suburban Florida girl who began receiving explicit love letters from a stranger in Greece.

programme.title: 48 Hours: The Secret Life of Eric Wright
programme.desc: In 1980, Kathi Spiars met a man who called himself Steve Marcum, and shortly married him.
programme.sub-title: The Secret Life of Eric Wright

programme.title: 48 Hours: For Love or Money?
programme.desc: Celeste Beard claims she is innocent of the murder of her husband Steven Beard, co-founder of an Austin television station. After a life of abuse and depression, Celeste married Beard. In October 1999, Beard was fatally shot while lying in bed.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Who Killed the Prosecutor?
programme.desc: Steve Tauzer, a prominent assistant district attorney in Kern County, CA, was found murdered in his garage. Chris Hillis, a former police officer, a colleague and friend of Tauzer, was arrested for the murder. Chris’ son Lance was key to the case.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Missing Molly
programme.desc: A report on the unsolved abduction and murder of 16-year-old Molly Bish. Bish disappeared in June 2000 from the local swimming hole. In May 2003, Tim McGuigan, a former police officer, helped discover evidence that lead to the discovery of Molly’s body.

programme.title: 48 Hours: A Family Accused
programme.desc: A report that focuses on cases where people accused of sexually abusing children were tried and imprisoned based on little real evidence other than the testimony of young children.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Blind
programme.desc: An F.B.I. man is blinded while investigating a crime syndicate attempting to take over a large chemical plant. Jim Phelps undergoes a delicate eye operation so as to appear convincingly blind when he poses as the sightless man.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Encore
programme.desc: William Shatner guest stars as an aging hoodlum who is artificially transplanted back to the 1930s and the scene of his unsolved crime.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: The Tram
programme.desc: The leaders of two underworld factions call a top-level meeting at a ski resort to set up a financial holding company capable of creating chaos within the national economy.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Mindbend
programme.desc: To help the IMF team break up a crime syndicate that specializes in brainwashing ex-convicts into committing political assassinations, Barney substitutes for a former prisoner looking for an operation to give himself a new appearance.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Shape-Up
programme.desc: Guest stars Gerald S. O'Loughlin and Christopher Stone portray crime syndicate bosses in charge of the waterfront who extort huge sums of money from every ship docking at a west coast port.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: The Miracle
programme.desc: An undercover agent is murdered while trying to intercept a huge heroin shipment in the Pacific Northwest and the IMF team is called to take over and put the dealer and his chief executioner out of business.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Encounter
programme.desc: Guest star Elizabeth Ashley plays the alcoholic wife of a crime syndicate leader whose involvement in an intimidation racket and the cold-blooded murder of an employee puts the IMF team on his trail.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Underwater
programme.desc: Stolen gems worth seventy-five million dollars scheduled to be fenced to an underworld figure suddenly disappear and the ship on which they were being transported is blown up at sea bringing the IMF team into the picture.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Invasion
programme.desc: Guest star Kevin McCarthy plays the role of a highly-placed Defense Department advisor who has murdered an Air Force official and sold vital top-secret material about America's nuclear defenses to foreign powers.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Blues
programme.desc: Barney poses as a prospective rock-music recording artist to get evidence linking an organized crime figurehead played by guest star William Windom to the sudden death of a beautiful recording star who was being blackmailed by the syndicate.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: The Visitors
programme.desc: Guest star Steve Forrest plays the role of a millionaire publisher and communications tycoon who has sold fifty-one percent of his empire to an underworld crime syndicate which gives them a powerful influence over state politics.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Nerves
programme.desc: Christopher George guest stars in the role of a psychotic ex-convict determined to wreak vengeance on society.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Run for the Money
programme.desc: Guest stars Richard Jaeckel and Herbert Edelman play gangsters who try to get control of all the illegal betting parlors in the country as well as a virtual takeover of all thoroughbred horse racing.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: The Connection
programme.desc: Jim Phelps and his IMF team are given the assignment of breaking an underworld ring attempting to set up one of the world's largest drug manufacturing plants off the coast of Africa.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: The Bride
programme.desc: Casey poses as a young peasant girl from Europe imported to be the bride of Joe Corvin, the top man at getting underworld millions into Swiss banks.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Stone Pillow
programme.desc: Guest star Bradford Dillman plays Edison, a former private detective turned extortionist who is blackmailing a powerful crime chief with a roll of film linking him to murder.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Image
programme.desc: Barney assumes the identity of a powerful psychic from a Caribbean island kingdom in order to hypnotize a defecting crime syndicate leader into revealing his super secret list of government officials on the take.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Committed
programme.desc: Nora Dawson, a witness to a murder, is committed to an asylum and is being systematically driven out of her mind with drugs when the IMF team is given twenty-four hours to save her life.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Bienvenido, Barney!
programme.desc: Baby Bop's friend Monty the butterfly passes through the park with a flock of friends on their way to Mexico. When Monty gets left behind by his insect friends and family, Barney takes everyone on a visit to Mexico.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Cousin Riff is moving to town. Barney encourages everyone to put on a special show.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney can't stop snacking on pistachios! Barney helps BJ explore the deeper meaning of team sports and friendships.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Seeing - Beethoven's Hear!
programme.desc: An ordinary game of "hide and seek" takes a not-so-ordinary turn when Baby Bop claims that an elephant is hiding in the park! Barney and Riff are happy to play along, but BJ thinks that his sister may be "seeing things."

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: When she finds out that Daniel is going on a trip the next day, Baby Bop wants to go on a trip as well. Everyone decides to pretend to travel the same way Daniel will travel.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: That Makes Me Mad!
programme.desc: Barney helps his young friends find good ways to express and deal with angry emotions.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: At Home in the Park
programme.desc: The children work together to make a bird house. They are happy to learn that the bird will have lots of company; many animals make their homes in the park too!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop has trouble following the rules and taking turns while playing games. BJ hurts his toe while pretending to be Captain Pickles.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Look What I Can Do
programme.desc: Barney's friends are playing a fun game of "I Can Do" where they each share something they can do. When BJ suggests having an "I Can Do" show, Baby Bop becomes worried.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: The Blame Game - Airplanes
programme.desc: Marcos accidentally breaks an art show project that Melanie has made. Afraid that she will be angry, he doesn't say anything, but then feels guilty. Barney helps him see that honesty and taking responsibility for what we do is always best. together.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: My Baby Brother
programme.desc: Barney reminds Baby Bop and BJ that although babies start out little, they grow fast. Through pretend play, favorite songs and fun games, they learn about the many ways we grow every day.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Movement - Separation
programme.desc: When some children in the park invite Barney to join them for a race, BJ and Baby Bop decide he'll need their help. They show him several ways to get ready for the big race by warming up and stretching.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Three kids try to build a pretend farm with disastrous results because they won't work together. Barney and his friends try to cheer up Olivia.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: As he watches birds fly overhead, Riff begins to daydream of flying himself. After seeing all the Moms playing with their kids in the Park, Baby Bop wonders what makes somebody a Mommy.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Venice, Anyone?
programme.desc: Emma can't play with her friends because she has to complete a school project about her family. Before you know it, Barney brings Emma on a trip to Venice to learn more about her great granddad.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ and Riff find a pirate map, but in order to find the treasure, they have to be able to count. It’s time for a music concert in the park.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop can't find anybody to play with her. When the others see her feeling sad, they decide to play whatever she wants to play. The dinos and kids are planning a camp-out at the park.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: A creepy spider inspires Barney to tell the story of “The Reluctant Dragon.”

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney and his friends are excited about a fishing contest. Barney tells Riff about a famous musician named Beethoven who wrote beautiful music even when he couldn't hear.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Come Blow Your Horn!
programme.desc: Barney and the kids are excited because five of Mr. Boyd's brass-playing buddies are putting on a show in the park tonight! Angela is learning to play the trumpet and knows that it takes a lot of practice. BJ arrives and the group visits the caboose.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: A Wonderful World of Colors and Shapes

programme.sub-title: A Wonderful World of Colors and Shapes

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney reminds Riff and BJ that Christmas is about more than getting gifts. Barney takes Melanie on a trip to the North Pole to deliver her letter in person.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop forgets to say "please" and "thank you". Riff shares his dream of becoming an inventor.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: A Fountain of Fun
programme.desc: While Barney and the children wait for Mr. Boyd's new fountain to be delivered, they have fun playing in and exploring the park.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Play It Safe!
programme.desc: It's always fun to share a day with Barney—but it's also important to learn how to "play it safe!" Baby Bop, wanting to be a good helper, has been attaching adhesive strips to her frustrated (and uninjured) brother, BJ.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: The Wild West
programme.desc: Allen and Ton explain their strategies at a storage auction in San Bernardino, win two units, then have a Pepperbox gun and vintage Lionel train set appraised by experts.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Ramped Up
programme.desc: Ton and Allen go head-to-head with their former assistant, Carolyn, while Ton battles a bad back. The guys discover an automatic BB gun and, when negotiations to sell a stunt ramp go south, Allen faces a tough decision.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Sin City Shootout
programme.desc: 1953 Chevy-inspired bumper car; scores of M16 30-round magazines; reproduction 1940s boardwalk photobooth; aerosol-powered spud gun potato launchers.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Wreck-Shaw
programme.desc: Allen drags a starving Ton to a weekend auction and some newbie buyers try to eat up all the profit. Will a rickshaw, a wrecking ball, and blast detonators be enough to satisfy Ton's appetite, or will the guys go home hungry?

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Ice, Ice, Baby
programme.desc: Ton and Allen have to get aggressive when they encounter a towering opponent with a mountain of cash. The guys uncover a vintage ice resurfacing machine and an Olympic biathlon rifle!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Everything's Bigger in Texas
programme.desc: Winchester 1886 rifle invented to kill buffalo; collection of 1950s electric Slot Cars.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Early Bird Special
programme.desc: Vintage Shooting Star carnival game; professional Mixed Martial Arts octagon fighting cage.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Trading Places
programme.desc: When an injury sidelines Allen, he stays at the shop while Ton takes Big Sis to the auction and goes rogue, ignoring Allen's advice and making risky purchases that could lose the partners a boatload of cash.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Catch My Drift?
programme.desc: The guys land a custom drift car and a luxury antiques unit that could have a one-of-a-kind pilot pistol from WWI. While trying to track down the provenance of the gun, Ton's lead foot gets them into trouble at their car sale.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Whiskey Business
programme.desc: An auction in Allen's old stomping grounds triggers memories of being a "lone wolf." The guys find a set of RC race boats and when they buy a man-cave unit full of old whiskey, a night of partying leads to some tough sales.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Reel Money
programme.desc: 1970's-era Fighting Chair used for big-game fishing; military MILES "laser tag" training system.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: The Chicago Grand Slam
programme.desc: 1984 Yamaha SS440 Snowmobile that introduced the revolutionary shock absorber suspension; rare autographed 1920s baseball glove; mint Hank Aaron signed baseball; authentic 1950s Robby the Robot collector toy.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Hall of Fame Game
programme.desc: Allen heads to an auction with Elle, while Ton and Big Sis revamp the shop to help bring in more customers. Allen unearths a Ouija board and a set of antique basketballs, and goes toe-to-toe with an NBA Hall of Famer.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: The Dallas Mavericks
programme.desc: Military-grade Tomahawk cruise missile jet engine; century-old handcrafted wild west cowboy saddle; 1960s high roller's gold money.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Party Gras
programme.desc: On a trip to New Orleans, Ton and Allen look to mix business with pleasure. They wage war with some locals at a tough auction, win a Mardi Gras unit that contains a confetti blaster and a modern calliope, then hit the town.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Tough as Nails
programme.desc: CJ seeks to disrupt Allen's search for serenity at a storage auction before the guy's head to a live auction to buy and sell. They uncover an unusual Marilyn Monroe statue, an industrial slip n' slide, and a bed of nails.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: To Pawn or Not To Pawn
programme.desc: On the brink of a major decision, Ton and Allen face-off with Carolyn at an auction in Covina. They win a "special effects unit" that contains big machines for crazy stunt work. The guys decide the fate of the shop.
programme.sub-title: To Pawn or Not To Pawn

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Allen's Got Balls
programme.desc: After returning to LA, Ton & Allen head deep into hostile auction hunter territory and butt heads with a familiar old foe! They discover a driving range golf ball picker vehicle and one of the most accurate rifles ever made!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Louisiana Purchase
programme.desc: In Louisiana, Ton and Allen encounter some salty locals at a shipping crate auction, and navigate choppy waters at a boat auction. After spending a lot of cash, they find a tactical blast shield and a bass fishing boat.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Sidecar Surprise
programme.desc: An unassuming mom in an oversized sweater tries to pull the wool over Ton & Allen's eyes at a storage auction in Arlington, VA. The guys uncover a black powder cannon designed in the 15th century & a Russian military motorcycle!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Drumming Up Cash
programme.desc: Ton & Allen face some silent but deadly competition at an auction in nearby Glendale. They discover a Zildjian brand percussion cymbal from the 1940's and a pyrotechnics control board designed for large-scale fireworks displays!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Viva la Vegas
programme.desc: 1974 death-defying Zig Zag magic sword box, an ultra-rare 1910 poster of the infamous magician Chung Ling Soo, an 1800s naval cane sword and century-old Metlach German beer steins.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Quadzilla Attacks!
programme.desc: Extreme Quadzilla ATV, the fastest 4 wheeler ever; a 1910s Remington Derringer and an 1880s Palm Squeezer pistol; revolutionary 1800s Western Electric telephone with a crank dial.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Disco and Dice
programme.desc: 1978 Williams Disco pinball machine; 1898 dice popper gambling device; talking 1990s bar top breathalyzer; deadly fishing harpoon.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Once a Pawn a Time
programme.desc: Ton and Allen discover that owning a pawn shop is way more trouble than they expected. With bills stacking up, even an antique ammo collection and some classic football helmets may not be enough to put the guys in the black.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Rock-et to Me!
programme.desc: Ton and Allen end up in a turf war at a Hollywood auction and at odds with each other over whether to gamble on what could be an antique slot machine. Allen attempts to fly a jetpack.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Big Easy Money
programme.desc: Allen & Ton head to New Orleans in search of easy money, but instead encounter some bayou bidders with big-time bucks! They discover a pair of early 19th-century dueling pistols & a vintage airboat fitted with a turbine engine!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Allen vs. Ton

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Great American Cashtime
programme.desc: Rare 1950s Master Pitch baseball machine; 1980s Jaws of Life tool used by rescuers to pry open cars.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Off the Deep End
programme.desc: Ton and Allen quarrel over bidding strategy at a rare auction on Catalina Island and must race against the clock to unload a one man submersible that could be worth big money… if they can make it work.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Fake Punt Payoff
programme.desc: Ton and Allen contend with a large crowd of inexperienced auction-buyers at a huge storage container auction in Pasadena. They find a small fleet of Segway personal transporters and an enormous custom-made shotgun.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Friends in Hawaii Places
programme.desc: Allen &Ton enlist an assistant, Carolyn, and Allen's Hawaiian buddy, Junior, to help them battle local buyers. The guys find a WWII-era motor scooter and a stash of antique Polynesian tattoo tools that get used on Ton!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Ton's Driller Instinct
programme.desc: Ton & Allen encounter aggressive outdoorsmen in the Alaskan wilderness. The guys discover an 8-wheeled ATV that travels on both land & water and an air percussion hammer that can drill through solid rock!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Always Money in Philadelphia

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Ton's Hot Commodity
programme.desc: Allen is late to the auction, leaving Ton to battle a feisty female auction buyer on his own! The guys find a fully functional flamethrower and a rare hand-carved bow and arrow set from South America!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Separation Anxiety
programme.desc: With Ton missing, Allen struggles to keep the pawn shop afloat while training Elle to work an auction. After a fierce bidding war with Caroline, pieces from a century old motorcycle may not be enough to save the day. ed.
